title: Blonde Brownies Take the Spotlight!
story: Blonde brownies get their moment to shine every January 22 on National Blonde Brownie Day! These golden treats aren't actually brownies at all - they're blondies, made with brown sugar and vanilla instead of chocolate. People celebrate by baking batches of these chewy, butterscotch-flavored squares and sharing them with friends and family. Some bakers add chocolate chips, nuts, or caramel swirls to make them extra special. Social media fills with photos of homemade blondies as people show off their favorite recipes. It's the perfect excuse to try something new in the kitchen and discover why these "blonde" treats have such a loyal following!
activity_question: What gives blonde brownies their golden color and butterscotch flavor?
bonus_fact: Blondies were actually invented before chocolate brownies!

title: Spice Up Your Life Day!
story: People across America celebrate National Hot Sauce Day every January 22 by adding fiery flavors to their favorite foods! Restaurants offer special spicy menu items, while families have hot sauce tasting contests at home. Some brave souls try the hottest sauces they can find, while others stick to mild varieties. Social media fills with photos of people's spiciest meals and collections of colorful hot sauce bottles. Many stores offer discounts on different brands, making it the perfect day to discover new flavors from around the world.
activity_question: What do families often do at home on National Hot Sauce Day?
bonus_fact: The hottest hot sauce can be over 1,000 times spicier than a jalapeño pepper!

title: Meow Means What? Cat Q&A Day!
story: Your cat has been trying to tell you something important, and January 22 is the perfect day to figure out what! Answer Your Cat's Questions Day encourages pet owners to pay extra attention to their feline friends and try to understand what they're really asking. Does that meow mean "feed me" or "pet me"? Is that head bump a question about playtime? Cat lovers spend the day observing their pets' behaviors, learning cat body language, and responding to every purr, chirp, and tail swish. Some people even have full conversations with their cats, pretending to translate their meows into actual questions about life, the weather, or why dogs exist!
activity_question: What do cat owners try to understand on Answer Your Cat's Questions Day?
activity_choices: What their cats are trying to communicate
How to teach cats to speak English
How to make cats do tricks
activity_answer: What their cats are trying to communicate
bonus_fact: Cats have over 16 different meow sounds, each with different meanings!

title: Celebration of Life Day: Party for Being Alive!
story: Every January 22, people around the world celebrate simply being alive on Celebration of Life Day! This special day encourages everyone to appreciate their own life and the lives of loved ones around them. People celebrate by doing things that make them happy - spending time with family, trying new activities, or helping others in their community. Some folks share gratitude posts on social media about what makes life wonderful. It's a perfect day to call someone you care about or do something that brings you joy!
activity_question: What do people often do to celebrate Celebration of Life Day?
activity_choices: Spend time with family and do things that make them happy
Hide indoors and avoid all people
Only eat birthday cake all day long
activity_answer: Spend time with family and do things that make them happy
follow_up_question: What activity makes you feel most grateful to be alive?

title: Spots of Fun on Polka Dot Day!
story: Polka dots take over the world every January 22 during National Polka Dot Day! People everywhere dig through their closets to find anything covered in those perfect little circles. Kids wear polka dot dresses, shirts, and even socks to school. Adults rock spotted ties, scarves, and accessories at work. Social media fills up with selfies of people showing off their dotted outfits. Some families bake polka dot cookies or cupcakes with white chocolate chips. Art teachers plan special dot-painting projects, and fashion lovers celebrate this timeless pattern that never goes out of style. It's amazing how such simple circles can make everyone smile!
activity_question: What do people commonly wear to celebrate National Polka Dot Day?
bonus_fact: The polka dot pattern got its name from the popular polka dance craze!

title: Come in From the Cold Day
story: Bundle up because January 22 is Come in From the Cold Day! This cozy holiday encourages people to invite friends, neighbors, or even strangers inside to warm up from winter's chill. People celebrate by opening their doors, sharing hot chocolate, soup, or warm drinks with anyone who needs to escape the cold. Some folks host impromptu gatherings or check on elderly neighbors to make sure they're staying warm and safe.
activity_question: What do people commonly share on Come in From the Cold Day?
follow_up_question: How could you help someone stay warm during winter in your neighborhood?
